content: Just as human language learners use deliberate written reflection (the pedagogical sense of reflexion from high school literacy curricula) to refine grammar and argument structure, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be structured around scaffolded, vocabulary-like iterative feedback cycles that ground abstract self-correction in concrete, labeled error "lexical items". This framing turns unstructured LLM self-reflection into a learnable literacy practice analogous to how students build writing proficiency through repeated guided reflexion exercises.
